title: Introduction
description: NOC 64100 – Retail salespersons and visual merchandisers | GTR Immigration Retail salespersons and visual merchandisers under NOC 64100 are the frontline of Canada's massive consumer economy. While salespersons drive revenue through expertise and customer service, visual merchandisers define the brand aesthetic through strategic store layouts and displays. As a TEER 4 occupation, this role is a highly accessible entry point for international workers, providing wide-ranging employment across all provinces and acting as a springboard for retail management careers.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level4Major Group64 – Sales and service representatives and other customer and personal services occupationsSub-Major Group641 – Sales and account representativesMinor Group6410 – Sales and account representativesNOC Code64100

duties: Greet customers and discuss the type, quality, and quantity of merchandise sought.
Advise customers on the use and care of merchandise and provide specialized advice.
Prepare merchandise for purchase, including processing payments and arranging delivery.
Design and set up window and in-store displays to promote merchandise (Visual Merchandisers).
Maintain sales records and assist in inventory management.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
For visual merchandisers, a college diploma in visual merchandising or a related field may be required.
On-the-job training is provided by most employers.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate a total of 200,000+ new job openings over the next decade due to expansion and high turnover, making this an extremely stable sector for initial entry into Canada.
labour_market_demand: The retail sector is one of Canada's largest employers. Demand is Very Good in urban centers like Toronto, Montreal, and Vancouver. As physical retail evolves into "experiential retail," the need for skilled visual merchandisers to create immersive store environments is at an all-time high.
Massive Job Volume: Retail vacancies are plentiful in every Canadian province.
Path to Management: Clear progression to Retail Supervisor (NOC 62010) and Manager (NOC 60020).
Workplace Culture: Diverse and inclusive environments with strong focus on customer service excellence.
pathways: Provincial Nominee Program (PNP): Entry-level and semi-skilled streams in Alberta, BC, and Saskatchewan.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A fast route for retail workers in the Maritimes.
Rural and Northern Immigration Pilot (RNIP): Addressing retail needs in smaller communities.
